characters.name: Nina Sayers
motivations: earn the lead role in Swan Lake
embody both the White and Black Swan

relationships.detail: Thomas pushes Nina to access raw emotion and sensuality she has suppressed
Lily represents the uninhibited darkness Nina must integrate
Erica infantilizes Nina to keep her dependent and controlled
Beth is the fading star Nina both idolizes and replaces

characters.name: Thomas Leroy
motivations: stage a transcendent production of Swan Lake
mold Nina into a complete artist
provoke authentic emotion in his dancers

relationships.detail: Uses psychological and physical provocation to break through Nina's rigidity
Discards Beth once her usefulness as prima ballerina ends
Embodies the dark, instinctive qualities Nina lacks and fears
Earns Thomas's approval through natural expressiveness

characters.name: Erica Sayers
motivations: prevent Nina from surpassing her own failed ambitions
maintain total emotional control over her daughter

relationships.detail: Keeps Nina in a childlike state through guilt and surveillance

characters.name: Beth MacIntyre
motivations: cling to her identity as prima ballerina

relationships.detail: Serves as a dark mirror of what total devotion to the role costs
Cast aside after years of service to his artistic vision

places.name: Lincoln Center Ballet Studio
places.description: The rehearsal studios where Nina has trained for years, a world of mirrors, barres, and relentless discipline where she excels at technical precision but struggles with emotional expression.

places.name: Nina and Erica's Apartment
places.description: A suffocating pink-walled bedroom filled with stuffed animals and music boxes, reflecting Erica's desire to keep Nina as a perpetual child. Both a refuge and a prison.

places.name: Lincoln Center Main Stage
places.description: The grand performance stage where Nina's opening night of Swan Lake takes place. The site of her ultimate triumph and self-destruction, where hallucination and reality merge completely.

places.name: Downtown Nightclub
places.description: The dark, pulsing club where Lily takes Nina to let loose. A liminal space where Nina first experiments with losing control, taking ecstasy, and confronting her repressed desires.

objects.name: Studio Mirrors
objects.description: The ever-present mirrors in the rehearsal studio and Nina's home that increasingly show distorted reflections, cracks, and her Black Swan alter ego staring back.
objects.significance: Externalize Nina's fracturing psyche and the duality she must integrate. Her reflections become autonomous, representing the shadow self demanding acknowledgment.
rules: Reflections increasingly diverge from Nina's actual movements
Cracks in mirrors parallel cracks in Nina's sanity
The Black Swan first manifests through mirror reflections

objects.name: Black Swan Costume
objects.description: The dark, feathered costume for the Odile role that Nina must wear for the seduction scene. Putting it on catalyzes her final transformation.
objects.significance: Represents the dark identity Nina must inhabit. Wearing it triggers the hallucinatory physical transformation where she sprouts black feathers and her legs bend backward.
rules: Donning the costume accelerates the psychological transformation
The costume blurs the line between performance and psychotic break

objects.name: Nail File / Glass Shard
objects.description: The sharp objects Nina uses to scratch and cut herself throughout the film, culminating in the mirror shard she believes she stabs Lily with but actually drives into her own abdomen.
objects.significance: Tracks Nina's escalating self-harm as a physical manifestation of her internal war. The final stabbing represents both the murder of her rigid old self and the mortal cost of transformation.
rules: Self-injury intensifies as the transformation progresses
The weapon turns inward — the enemy was always herself

moments.label: The Perfect White Swan
moments.description: Nina is a technically flawless but emotionally repressed ballet dancer living under her mother's total control. She practices obsessively, sleeps in a child's bedroom, and has never deviated from rigid discipline. She is the ideal White Swan — pure, fragile, obedient — but cannot access the dark sensuality of the Black Swan.

character_states.char_nina: repressed, technically perfect, emotionally stunted, desperate for the lead role
character_states.char_erica: controlling, living vicariously, maintaining Nina's childlike dependency

transitions.description: Nina resolves to win the dual role of Odette/Odile in the new Swan Lake production, staking her entire identity on achieving perfection

moments.label: Cast as the Swan Queen
moments.description: Thomas casts Nina as the Swan Queen after she bites him during an aggressive audition — the first crack in her controlled facade. He tells her she must find the Black Swan within herself. Simultaneously, Lily arrives at the company, embodying everything Nina suppresses: spontaneity, sensuality, and raw instinct.

character_states.char_nina: elated but terrified, aware she cannot yet embody the Black Swan
character_states.char_thomas: calculating, testing whether Nina can transcend her limitations
character_states.char_lily: effortlessly expressive, unaware she represents Nina's shadow

transitions.description: Wins the Swan Queen part but only after an uncharacteristic act of aggression — biting Thomas — that hints at buried darkness
Beth is forcibly retired, foreshadowing the cost of total identification with a role

moments.label: Resisting the Dark Side
moments.description: Nina tries to access the Black Swan through her usual method — more practice, more control, more perfection — but Thomas tells her she is frigid and mechanical. She begins experiencing hallucinations: scratches on her back, her reflection moving independently, skin peeling to reveal feathers. Her mother tightens control. Nina resists every avenue of transformation.

character_states.char_nina: frustrated, frightened by hallucinations, doubling down on control
character_states.char_thomas: disappointed, pushing harder, questioning his casting choice
character_states.char_erica: alarmed by Nina's stress, tightening surveillance

transitions.description: Realizes that technical perfection alone cannot produce the Black Swan — something fundamental in her must change
Hallucinations begin: scratches appear on her back, her reflection moves independently in mirrors

moments.label: Night of Abandon
moments.description: Lily takes Nina to a nightclub where Nina takes ecstasy, drinks heavily, and for the first time in her life abandons control. She hallucinates a sexual encounter with Lily. The next morning she oversleeps, arrives late to rehearsal, and discovers Lily learning her part. Reality and hallucination become indistinguishable. Nina exists between her old identity and something new and terrifying.

character_states.char_nina: unmoored, paranoid, oscillating between euphoria and terror
character_states.char_lily: casual and unbothered, unaware of Nina's psychological crisis

transitions.description: Crosses into liminal territory — no longer the controlled perfectionist but not yet the integrated artist, trapped between identities
The imagined sexual encounter with Lily represents Nina's psyche attempting to merge with her shadow self

moments.label: Opening Night Collapse
moments.description: On opening night, Nina's psychosis reaches its peak. She stumbles during the White Swan pas de deux with her partner. Backstage, she confronts Lily — or a hallucination of Lily — in her dressing room and stabs her with a mirror shard to prevent her from taking the role. She hides the body and returns to stage, but the crucible is the realization that she has committed an irreversible act to protect her transformation.

character_states.char_nina: psychotic, desperate, willing to kill to complete the transformation
character_states.char_lily: alive and unharmed in reality — Nina stabbed herself

transitions.description: Chooses to destroy what she perceives as the external threat to her role, not yet understanding the enemy is internal
The stabbing reveals that Nina's transformation requires self-destruction — the shard went into her own body

moments.label: Becoming the Black Swan
moments.description: Nina returns to the stage for the Black Swan act. In a hallucinatory crescendo, her arms sprout black feathers, her eyes turn red, her legs bend into avian form. She dances the Black Swan with terrifying, transcendent power — feral, seductive, and utterly unleashed. The audience erupts. Thomas whispers that she was perfect. For one luminous moment, Nina has integrated both halves: technical mastery and raw, dark emotion.

character_states.char_nina: transcendent, fully embodying both White and Black Swan, bleeding from the hidden wound
character_states.char_thomas: awestruck, witnessing the artistic perfection he demanded

transitions.description: Achieves total integration of light and dark selves in her performance, becoming the complete artist at the cost of her sanity and her life
Delivers a performance of supernatural power that fulfills Thomas's vision and transcends her own limitations

moments.label: It Was Perfect
moments.description: Nina performs the final White Swan death scene — Odette's suicide leap — and collapses on the mattress below the stage. The company discovers she is bleeding from a real wound: the mirror shard in her abdomen. As Thomas and the others rush to her, Nina whispers 'I felt it. Perfect. It was perfect.' She has achieved the new identity — the complete artist who can embody both purity and darkness — but the transformation was literal self-destruction. The screen whites out as the audience gives a standing ovation.

character_states.char_nina: dying, ecstatic, at peace — the transformation is complete but fatal
character_states.char_thomas: horrified at the physical cost, awed by the artistic achievement
character_states.char_lily: alive, confused, unaware she was the object of Nina's psychotic projection

transitions.description: Completes her transformation into the perfect artist, achieving a new identity that fuses discipline and abandon — but the cost is her life
The mortal wound from the mirror shard — self-inflicted in her psychotic state — bleeds out during the final act, making the stage death real
